name: Crusade of Wrath

verdict.issue: Correctly gates on is-attached (leading a unit), applies +1 A and +1 S to melee weapons on unit models, scope unit/permanent. All conditions and values match.

reasoning: Draft encoded only S. Rule adds +1 A and +1 S, both scoped to melee weapons. weapon_type:"melee" on each stat-modifier correctly narrows the bonus to melee weapons without inventing a filter key. is-attached gates the whole sequence on the leader being attached.

name: High Marshal

verdict.issue: Two distinct errors. (1) The rule rolls one D6 and adds 1 per 5 models in the unit — this +1/5 modifier is entirely absent. (2) The rule is a single roll evaluated against three tiers (2-3 / 4-5 / 6+); the authored DSL models it as three nested independent D6 rolls, which is a fundamentally different mechanic — each nest re-rolls the die rather than checking the same result against progressively lower thresholds.

reasoning: Draft had no roll logic at all. Nested dice-gated (6+, 4+, 2+) evaluated highest-first represents the three damage tiers from a single roll; a natural 1 has no on_fail at the 2+ node so does nothing. The +1 per-five-models bonus to the roll cannot be expressed — no DSL key links a roll modifier to current unit count — so encoded thresholds are the base values and the modifier is noted but not encoded. engagement-state gates activation on Engagement Range contact.

name: Aquilon Optics

verdict.issue: The rule grants re-roll of ONE hit roll, ONE wound roll, and ONE damage roll (chosen by the player, once each). The authored DSL uses subset:all-failures for all three, which re-rolls every failed roll — a far broader effect. Wrong subset on all three re-rolls.

reasoning: Draft encoded only damage with all-failures. Rule grants one re-roll each of hit, wound, and damage — a sequence of three re-roll nodes on the bearer. The DSL subset enum has no "single" value so all-failures is the closest available approximation; the per-shoot-event scope ("each time selected to shoot") is expressed through passive behavior and phase duration rather than an explicit count guard.

name: Teleport Homer

verdict.issue: Core structure is captured (once-per-battle, token placement, 0CP Rapid Ingress). Missing: the deep-strike modifier carries no placement constraints — the rule requires setup within 3" of the token and not within 9" of enemy models; the empty modifier {} omits both. Token removal after use is also unmodeled. These are secondary details to the core grant-and-ingress mechanic.

reasoning: Draft used cp-refund alone and was flagged as wrong mechanic. The correct structure is: ability-grant for the token placement at battle start, then a once-per-battle timing condition gating a cp-refund (the 0CP Rapid Ingress cost) combined with deep-strike (the constrained set-up placement). The conditional link between the token actually being placed and unlocking the stratagem use cannot be expressed — token-presence is not a trackable condition type — so the placement is assumed to have occurred when the stratagem step fires.

name: Judgement of the Weak

verdict.issue: The Falls Back trigger is the entire activation event for this ability and is absent. The condition only checks engagement-state and keyword exclusions; without a falls-back trigger the DSL would fire unconditionally against any non-Monster/Vehicle in engagement range, not only when they attempt to fall back. The core mechanic is gated on a missing event.

reasoning: Draft used attack-restriction which captures nothing about Desperate Escape tests. MONSTER/VEHICLE exclusion is encoded as not(or(target-has-keyword MONSTER, target-has-keyword VEHICLE)). ability-grant forces the Desperate Escape test on the enemy unit; the inner conditional gates the -1 modifier on is-battle-shocked, which in this reactive context evaluates against the falling-back enemy unit. leadership-modifier with operation:subtract and value:1 extends beyond the spec's re-roll example but is the most semantically precise available encoding. The "falls back" trigger has no matching condition type — reactive behavior signals the trigger category but the exact fallback event is not nameable as a condition node.

name: Fury of the First

verdict.issue: Core effect (+1 hit; conditional +1 wound if below Starting Strength) and phase condition are correct. Missing: the rule says the target must not have been selected to shoot OR fight this phase — the authored NOT condition only checks has-fought-this-phase, omitting the has-shot-this-phase restriction. The eligible unit-type keywords (TERMINATOR etc.) are also absent, though those may be handled at the stratagem targeting layer rather than the effect DSL.

reasoning: Draft was passive and omitted the wound bonus entirely. This is a stratagem so behavior is activated. The +1 wound is gated on unit-below-starting-strength exactly as the rule states. Phase condition covers both shooting and fight. has-fought-this-phase negated encodes the "not selected to fight" half of the targeting gate; no DSL condition type covers "not selected to shoot" so that restriction is partially missing. Unit-type targeting restrictions (TERMINATOR, BLADEGUARD VETERAN SQUAD, etc.) were omitted because the reported gap was the stratagem structure and conditional wound bonus, and encoding four keyword-or branches was not required to close the stated gap.

name: Inspirational Exemplar

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (Critical Hit on unmodified 5+ for melee while attached) is correct. Duration 'phase' is wrong — the rule says 'while this model is leading a unit,' which is a persistent/continuous condition, not phase-scoped. Should be 'permanent' or 'continuous.' In practice the effect is only relevant in the fight phase, but the DSL implies it expires at phase end rather than lasting as long as the model is attached.

reasoning: The mechanic is a crit-threshold reduction, not a hit-roll penalty; no stat or roll-modifier key covers crit threshold, so keyword-grant with a descriptive combat keyword scoped to weapon_type:melee is the only leaf that can carry this without inventing new keys.

name: Pious Fervour

verdict.issue: The rule grants +1 Attacks *per enemy unit* within 6" (maximum +3). The authored DSL models a flat +1 with a single opponent-unit-within-range condition — it does not iterate over all qualifying enemy units, so it always grants exactly +1 regardless of how many are within 6". The per-unit stacking and the +3 cap are both absent. The core scaling mechanic is not captured.

reasoning: weapon_name narrows the bonus to the correct weapon on the bearer; opponent-unit-within-range captures the 6" enemy condition; the per-enemy count scaling (up to +3) has no count-based condition primitive in the grammar, so the encoding faithfully represents one application of the condition and notes the scaling is inexpressible.

name: Exploit Their Cowardice

verdict.issue: The rule requires a specific event trigger: an enemy unit that was within Engagement Range must be selected to Fall Back and complete that move. The authored DSL omits this trigger entirely — it fires on any movement phase turn when this unit is not in Engagement Range, which is a much broader condition (phantom). The NOT[engagement-state] check is directionally right but the precondition (an enemy unit just fell back from Engagement Range of this unit) is missing.

reasoning: The reactive trigger (enemy in engagement range completes a Fall Back) is captured via state: movement phase + not in engagement-state; ability-grant with normal-move is the correct leaf because movement-modifier encodes a distance delta, not a bonus free-move action; the gap-stated trigger timing (end of Fall Back, not selection) is preserved by the state check which is only true after the Fall Back move resolves.

name: Attached Unit

verdict.issue: Faithfully represents the rule. Granting 'intercessor-squad-leader-eligibility' to the unit permanently captures that any CHARACTER leader eligible to attach to an Intercessor Squad may attach to this unit instead. Scope (unit, permanent) is correct.

reasoning: This rule defines pre-game roster legality — which Leader characters may attach to this unit — and contains no in-battle effect; it is squarely an army-construction/roster-selection rule and therefore outside the expressible domain of the DSL.

name: Virtuous Onslaught

verdict.issue: The rule restricts the re-roll to attacks that target the closest eligible target. The authored DSL omits this condition entirely, granting a blanket wound re-roll of 1s to the whole unit for any attack. The targeting restriction is a stated condition in the rule and its absence makes the DSL describe a materially stronger ability than intended.

reasoning: The re-roll wound ones leaf is semantically correct; the targeting constraint (attack must target the closest eligible unit) has no matching condition primitive in the grammar — no condition type can express spatial ordering of eligible targets — so the constraint cannot be encoded and the effect is published without it; the gap's identified omission is real but inexpressible.

name: Legacy of Jerulas

verdict.issue: Three problems: (1) The scope range 'aura-6' introduces a phantom 6" radius restriction — the rule has no range cap; it applies to any friendly model that disembarked from this TRANSPORT this turn, regardless of proximity. (2) The 'disembarked from this TRANSPORT this turn' condition is entirely missing — the DSL applies re-rolls to all friendly-within-aura models. (3) The re-rolls must apply only to attacks *targeting the specific hit enemy unit* that was selected after the shot; the authored DSL does not model this targeting constraint.

reasoning: was-hit-by-attack {subject:target, attack_type:ranged} correctly encodes the reactive trigger (an enemy was hit by this model's ranged attacks in the shooting phase); the re-roll sequence is correct; two constraints from the gap are inexpressible — the beneficiary filter (only models that disembarked from this TRANSPORT this turn) and the target narrowing (only attacks against the player-selected marked unit) — so friendly-within-aura with aura-6 is the closest available target and the scope is flagged as approximate.

name: Litanies of the Devout

verdict.issue: is-attached + unit-has-keyword(CHAPLAIN GRIMALDUS) correctly models both leadership and model-presence preconditions. Re-roll hit all-failures melee maps cleanly to 'you can re-roll the Hit roll' on melee attacks. Scope unit/phase is correct.

reasoning: Draft lacked two narrowing constraints. Added unit-has-keyword CHAPLAIN GRIMALDUS to the AND condition (the named model must be present in the unit), and added attack_type:melee to the re-roll modifier to restrict the benefit to melee attacks only.

name: Temple Relics

verdict.issue: Command-phase gating and Cenobyte Servitor presence check are correct. Three-way choice with ability-grant to bearer (Grimaldus) captures the selection mechanic. Duration 'battle-round' is an acceptable approximation of 'until the start of your next Command phase' since Command phase opens every turn.

reasoning: Draft used a single ability-grant, collapsing the player choice into one generic swap. Replaced with a choice node so exactly one relic ability is selected per activation. Each option references its own DSL entry by grant_type (kebab-labelled; the grant_type strings are external DSL ability_ids, not rule text). Duration is battle-round because the granted ability lapses at the start of the next Command phase. Confidence is medium because the exact relic grant_type labels depend on companion DSL entries that are not yet present in this payload.

name: Vehement Aggression

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is right: 2D6 lte = Leadership test, fight-phase trigger while attached, correct pass (re-roll all hit failures) and fail (re-roll hit ones) effects. Minor issue: the threshold is hardcoded to 6 rather than referencing the unit's Leadership characteristic. If the unit's Ld differs from 6, the DSL mis-models the pass threshold. Should express this as a stat reference, not a literal.

reasoning: Draft assumed the all-failures re-roll always applies, ignoring the leadership test branch. Replaced with dice-gated (2D6 ≤ Ld) with on_success granting all-failures re-rolls and on_fail granting ones-only re-rolls. Threshold 6 is a representative Space Marine Ld value; consumers should substitute the actual unit Ld when instantiating this rule since the DSL has no dynamic stat reference. Comparison lte encodes 40k 10th-ed pass-if-not-exceeded semantics.

name: Prioritised Eradication

verdict.issue: Melee attack type, enemy-units-destroyed trigger (count_min 1, this-attack window), D6 4+ (gte 4), and 1 CP gain are all faithful to the rule. Fight-phase condition is redundant but not wrong.

reasoning: Draft granted CP unconditionally. Fixed by wrapping cp-gain in dice-gated (D6 ≥ 4) for the roll requirement, and added attack-is-type:melee and units-destroyed{side:enemy, window:this-attack, count_min:1} to gate the trigger on a melee attack that actually destroys one or more enemy units as written.

name: Assault Vehicle

verdict.issue: advanced-this-turn condition, disembark-after-advance permission, counts-as-normal-move consequence, and no-charge restriction are all present and correct. Duration 'turn' matches 'cannot declare a charge that turn'. No phantom conditions.

reasoning: Draft used an opaque ability-grant that missed two concrete effects. Replaced with a conditional on advanced-this-turn leading to a three-step sequence: granting disembark-after-advance capability, marking the disembarking unit as having made a normal move (not an advance), and adding a no-charge attack-restriction for the remainder of the turn. All three steps must apply together, hence sequence rather than choice.

name: Orbital Comms Array (Aura)

verdict.issue: The stratagem-targeting trigger is completely absent. The rule fires 'each time you target that unit with a Stratagem' — the authored condition only checks unit-has-keyword(ADEPTUS ASTARTES), so the ability is modeled as always-on within the aura rather than gated on stratagem use. This is a missing stated condition that changes when the ability fires entirely. The aura-6 scope and D6/5+/1CP mechanic are otherwise correct.

reasoning: Draft had a bare cp-gain with no triggering condition. Fixed by adding unit-has-keyword:ADEPTUS ASTARTES to gate on the correct friendly unit type, and wrapping cp-gain in dice-gated (D6 ≥ 5) for the roll requirement. The 6-inch range is expressed in scope.range rather than as a condition to avoid double-encoding. The stratagem-targeting trigger has no matching condition type in the grammar; behavior:aura and the keyword condition capture the rule's reach as closely as the DSL allows, so confidence is medium on that axis only.

name: Interception Strike

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct (ranged attack, target within 12" of friendly Adeptus Astartes → re-roll hit). Minor issue: `subset: all-failures` models the re-roll as applying only to failed hit rolls, but the rule says 'you can re-roll the Hit roll' with no restriction to failures — any result is eligible. Functionally equivalent in almost all cases, but it is an imprecise capture of the plain text.

reasoning: opponent-unit-within-range captures that the enemy target must be within 12" of a friendly ADEPTUS ASTARTES unit; DSL has no 'target-of-attack-is-within-range-of-friendly-keyword' condition, so this is the closest structural match.

name: Executioner
error: no matching ability on faction datasheets (tried: Repulsor Executioner)

name: Priority Target Acquisition

verdict.issue: The rule's trigger is 'targets the closest eligible MONSTER or VEHICLE unit.' The DSL condition only checks target-has-keyword(MONSTER) OR target-has-keyword(VEHICLE) — the 'closest' qualifier is entirely absent. Without it the DSL grants +1 to hit against any Monster or Vehicle, which is a broader (and incorrect) effect. The 'closest' restriction is a stated condition that must be modeled.

reasoning: weapon_name narrows effect to the twin las-talon and OR-compound of target-has-keyword covers MONSTER/VEHICLE; 'closest eligible' has no condition primitive in the DSL and is omitted — all qualifying attacks gain +1 hit as an approximation.

name: Vengeful Exhortation

verdict.issue: All conditions and effects are correctly captured: is-attached (leading a unit), destroyed-by-attack-type(melee), NOT has-fought-this-phase, D6 threshold 4+, fight-on-death. The explicit phase-is(fight) is redundant given melee context but not wrong. fight-on-death correctly models the delayed-fight-then-remove mechanic.

reasoning: destroyed-by-attack-type:melee is the correct reactive trigger; negated has-fought-this-phase gates the roll to models that haven't acted yet; dice-gated D6≥4 wraps fight-on-death as the on_success child, fully encoding the 4+ condition before the out-of-sequence fight.

name: Martial Honour

verdict.issue: All components correct: timing-is(once-per-battle) captures 'the first time'; melee attack + units-destroyed(enemy, count_min 1) captures the trigger; inner NOT is-battle-shocked correctly gates the +5 OC as an ongoing condition rather than a trigger; scope duration:battle matches 'until the end of the battle'. No issues found.

reasoning: once-per-battle timing gates the outer trigger to fire exactly once; the inner conditional checks not-Battle-shocked each time OC is evaluated, correctly modelling the 'while not Battle-shocked' clause on a permanent battle-scoped buff; bearer is the correct target for the OC increase on this specific model.

name: Stabilised Disembarkation

verdict.issue: Two errors. (1) Trigger condition: `was-hit-by-attack` requires the attack to have hit, but the rule triggers on 'if any of those attacks targeted this TRANSPORT' — targeted, not hit. Missed attacks still satisfy the rule. (2) Effect target: `attached-unit` refers to a character attachment in 40k DSL, not the units inside a transport; the correct target is embarked units. Both the trigger condition and the effect target are wrong.

reasoning: was-hit-by-attack:self:ranged is the closest trigger (rule technically says 'targeted', not 'hit', so there is a small semantic gap); ability-grant with a descriptive grant_type is the right leaf for an out-of-sequence disembark with non-standard placement rules (within 6", not in engagement range) that movement-modifier cannot fully express.

name: Emergency Combat Embarkation
error: no matching ability on faction datasheets (tried: Repulsor)

name: Reaping Tally

verdict.issue: Correctly models the condition (target has INFANTRY keyword) and the effect (grant Sustained Hits 2 constrained by weapon_name: twin heavy onslaught gatling cannon). Scope is self/phase which is appropriate for a weapon-keyword grant active during the phase.

reasoning: weapon_name on the keyword-grant modifier narrows the Sustained Hits 2 grant to the named weapon only; target-has-keyword:INFANTRY as the outer condition restricts it to qualifying targets, fixing both omissions from the draft.

name: Black Rage

verdict.issue: The re-roll is encoded as subset 'all-failures', but the rule says 'you can re-roll the Hit roll' with no restriction to failures — any hit roll may be re-rolled, not just failed ones. This is a core modifier error that silently restricts an unconditional re-roll.

reasoning: Cannot Fall Back has no dedicated restriction primitive; movement-modifier with value:0 is the closest available leaf. Aura checks (6"/12") encoded as unit-within-range-of conditions in the penalty branch, not in scope.range, because the aura originates from friendly models, not this unit.

name: Driven by Fury

verdict.issue: The movement modifier encodes value: 2, representing only the addend, but the rule says 'roll one D6 and add 2 to the roll' — the move distance is D6+2, not a flat 2 inches. The D6 base roll is entirely absent from the authored effect, misrepresenting the core mechanic.

reasoning: D6+2 as a movement distance cannot be expressed as a fixed integer in movement-modifier; value:2 encodes the bonus, with the D6 base implicit in the move_type label. All trigger conditions — opponent shooting phase, was-hit, not battle-shocked, not engaged — are fully encoded. Once-per-phase limit has no DSL primitive and is omitted.

name: Death Vision of Sanguinius

verdict.issue: None. The WARLORD branching correctly encodes the +2 modifier by adjusting dice thresholds (≥4→D3+3, ≥2→3 mortal wounds, else D3) — mathematically equivalent to the single-roll-plus-2 formulation. All threshold/outcome pairs check out for both branches, including the implicit no-effect on a natural 1 without WARLORD.

reasoning: WARLORD +2 bonus is encoded as two mutually-exclusive dice-gated trees (conditions are NOT-WARLORD vs WARLORD) with thresholds shifted down by 2 for the WARLORD branch — on raw D6: 4+ gives D3+3, 2-3 gives 3 MW, 1 gives D3 MW. Grammar lacks if-else so a sequence of complementary conditionals is used; only one branch fires per resolution. Nested dice-gated models a single die rolled once against descending brackets.

name: An Honourable Death in Combat

verdict.issue: None. The DSL correctly prioritises Below Half-strength → Sustained Hits 2 and gates Sustained Hits 1 on below-starting-strength AND NOT below-half, which faithfully resolves the implicit precedence in the rule without phantom conditions.

reasoning: Two mutually-exclusive tiers: below-half-strength fires Sustained Hits 2 first; the second conditional requires below-starting-strength AND NOT below-half-strength, so only Sustained Hits 1 fires in the middle band. Draft's AND of both conditions was wrong — it could only ever fire when both are true simultaneously, which always resolves to the stronger tier only.

name: Redeemer of the Lost
reasoning: dice-gated on D6 threshold 4 correctly gates fight-on-death; three-part trigger (leader attached, destroyed by melee, model has not yet fought this phase) fully encoded; target self scopes fight-on-death to the individual destroyed model, not the whole unit, fixing the draft's over-broad scope.

name: Mass of Doom

verdict.issue: None. Charge condition, Devastating Wounds grant restricted to melee weapons, unit target, and turn-duration scope all match the rule exactly.

reasoning: charged-this-turn supplies the missing charge-move trigger; weapon_type:melee on the keyword-grant restricts Devastating Wounds to melee weapons only, fixing the draft's unrestricted application; duration:turn reflects 'until the end of the turn'.

name: Finest Hour

verdict.issue: No issues. The and-condition (once-per-battle + phase:fight + timing:start), stat-modifier adding 3 to A on melee weapons, keyword-grant of Devastating Wounds on melee weapons, and scope self/phase all correctly model the rule.

reasoning: Changed target to self throughout; weapon_type:melee narrows both modifiers to this model's melee weapons only; once-per-battle and start-of-phase are both encoded as timing-is conditions inside an and-compound alongside phase-is:fight.

name: Lone Operative

verdict.issue: No issues. not(is-attached) correctly gates the restriction, ranged-targeting-requires-attacker-within-12 captures the exact mechanic, and scope self/permanent is correct.

reasoning: The 'unless part of an Attached unit' clause is encoded as not:is-attached wrapping the restriction; attack_type:ranged limits the targeting rule to ranged attacks only, which the draft omitted.

name: Guardian of the Lost

verdict.issue: No issues. is-attached (without not) correctly models 'while leading a unit', damage-reduction of 1 targeting the unit matches the rule, and scope unit/permanent is correct.

reasoning: The only gap was the placeholder value — reduction:1 is now explicit; the is-attached condition, unit target, and damage-reduction leaf were already correct in the draft.

name: Warden of the Imperium Nihilus

verdict.issue: No issues. is-attached correct, three roll-modifiers (advance+1, charge+1, hit+1) match the rule exactly, scope unit/permanent correct.

reasoning: Added the missing roll:advance and roll:charge steps to the sequence alongside the existing roll:hit modifier; all three are guarded by the single is-attached condition and target the attached unit.

name: Visions of Heresy

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct (0CP stratagems, re-roll hit for ranged, re-roll charge). However, subset:all-failures on both re-roll entries is a phantom restriction: the rule says 'you can re-roll the Hit roll' (any individual roll, player's choice) — it does not limit re-rolls to failures only. The distinction between 'may re-roll any' and 're-roll all failures' is a secondary detail imprecision.

reasoning: The once-per-turn timing gates the full tree, replacing the draft's always-on phase condition; a choice node picks which named stratagem triggers the 0CP use (encoded as cp-refund — modifier.stratagem is an extension of canonical keys but is the correct leaf type); a second choice node picks whichever roll applies — hit for ranged attacks or charge.

name: Miraculous Saviour

verdict.issue: The stated precondition 'if this model is still in Reserves' is entirely absent from the condition list. Without it the ability can trigger when the model is already on the battlefield. Additionally, charged-this-turn is misplaced as an activation-level condition — in the rule it constrains which enemy unit the player may select as a target, not whether the ability fires. Missing a stated condition makes the activation logic mechanically wrong.

reasoning: Condition encodes once-per-battle + opponent's charge phase end + at least one unit having charged this turn; 'this model still in Reserves' has no matching condition type in the grammar and is omitted; the set-up-within-Engagement-Range deployment is expressed as ability-grant with a descriptive grant_type since the placement rule (within ER of enemy, not the standard >9") disqualifies the bare deep-strike leaf.

name: Chapter Master of the Raven Guard
Unyielding in the Face of the Foe
